title: arenaria armerina var. frigida
intro: Arenaria armerina var. frigida, also known as the Frigid Sea Pink, is a low-growing, perennial plant species that belongs to the carnation family and is native to the mountainous regions of the Pyrenees. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which are thought to aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and fighting infections. The health benefits of Arenaria armerina var. frigida are attributed to its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce pain and inflammation, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and topical applications, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
